content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA with NNN integers. You have to tell Chef whether this array is closed under multiplication.

We say that an array is closed under multiplication if, for all sequences 1‚Č§S1<S2<‚čĮ<Sk‚Č§N1 \leq S_1 \lt S_2 \lt \dots \lt S_k \leq N1‚Č§S1‚Äč<S2‚Äč<‚čĮ<Sk‚Äč‚Č§N,  there exists 1‚Č§x‚Č§N1 \leq x \leq N1‚Č§x‚Č§N such that AS1‚čÖAS2‚čÖ‚čĮ‚čÖASk=AxA_{S_1} \cdot A_{S_2} \cdot \dots \cdot A_{S_k} = A_xAS1‚Äč‚Äč‚čÖAS2‚Äč‚Äč‚čÖ‚čĮ‚čÖASk‚Äč‚Äč=Ax‚Äč.

Output 1 if it is closed, and 0 otherwise.


Input Format

The first line of the input contains TTT - the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer - NNN
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers - the ithi^{th}ith of which is AiA_iAi‚Äč


Output Format
For each test case, output the answer on a new line. Output 111 if AAA is closed under multiplication, and 000 otherwise.
constraints: The sum of NNN across all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: [1,0,3][1, 0, 3][1,0,3] is closed under multiplication.
A1‚čÖA2=1‚čÖ0=0=A2A_1 \cdot A_2 = 1 \cdot 0 = 0 = A_2A1‚Äč‚čÖA2‚Äč=1‚čÖ0=0=A2‚Äč
A1‚čÖA2‚čÖA3=1‚čÖ0‚čÖ3=0=A2A_1 \cdot A_2 \cdot A_3 = 1 \cdot 0 \cdot 3 = 0 = A_2A1‚Äč‚čÖA2‚Äč‚čÖA3‚Äč=1‚čÖ0‚čÖ3=0=A2‚Äč
A1‚čÖA3=1‚čÖ3=3=A3A_1 \cdot A_3 = 1 \cdot 3 = 3 = A_3A1‚Äč‚čÖA3‚Äč=1‚čÖ3=3=A3‚Äč
A2‚čÖA3=0‚čÖ3=0=A2A_2 \cdot A_3 = 0 \cdot 3 = 0 = A_2A2‚Äč‚čÖA3‚Äč=0‚čÖ3=0=A2‚Äč
Therefore, the answer for the first test case is 111.
Test case 222: [1,‚ąí1,‚ąí1][1, -1, -1][1,‚ąí1,‚ąí1] is closed under multiplication.
A1‚čÖA2=1‚čÖ‚ąí1=‚ąí1=A2A_1 \cdot A_2 = 1 \cdot -1 = -1 = A_2A1‚Äč‚čÖA2‚Äč=1‚čÖ‚ąí1=‚ąí1=A2‚Äč
A1‚čÖA2‚čÖA3=1‚čÖ‚ąí1‚čÖ‚ąí1=1=A1A_1 \cdot A_2 \cdot A_3 = 1 \cdot -1 \cdot -1 = 1 = A_1A1‚Äč‚čÖA2‚Äč‚čÖA3‚Äč=1‚čÖ‚ąí1‚čÖ‚ąí1=1=A1‚Äč
A1‚čÖA3=1‚čÖ‚ąí1=‚ąí1=A2A_1 \cdot A_3 = 1 \cdot -1 = -1 = A_2A1‚Äč‚čÖA3‚Äč=1‚čÖ‚ąí1=‚ąí1=A2‚Äč
A2‚čÖA3=‚ąí1‚čÖ‚ąí1=1=A1A_2 \cdot A_3 = -1 \cdot -1 = 1 = A_1A2‚Äč‚čÖA3‚Äč=‚ąí1‚čÖ‚ąí1=1=A1‚Äč
Therefore, the answer for the second test case is 111.
Test case 333: A2‚čÖA3=3‚čÖ3=9A_2 \cdot A_3 = 3 \cdot 3 = 9A2‚Äč‚čÖA3‚Äč=3‚čÖ3=9 is not present in the array. Therefore, [1,3,3][1, 3, 3][1,3,3] is not closed under multiplication, and the answer is 000.
